basic_details.job_title: Goa PSC Recruitment 2026 - Apply Online for 25 Assistant Engineer, Psychiatrist and More Posts
basic_details.department: Department of Environment and Climate Change; Public Health Department (Goa Dental College & Hospital and Institute of Psychiatry and Human Behaviour); Town and Country Planning Department; Department of Personnel
basic_details.short_information: Goa Public Service Commission Recruitment 2026: Get the latest GPSC recruitment notification 2026 for various posts under Advertisement No. 05 Year 2026 published on 08.05.2026. The posts include vacancies across the Department of Environment and Climate Change, Public Health Department (Goa Dental College and Hospital and Institute of Psychiatry and Human Behaviour), Town and Country Planning Department, and Department of Personnel. The last date to apply online for all posts other than transfer on deputation is 22nd May 2026, and for transfer on deputation posts is 22nd June 2026. Apply thro

important_dates.interview_date: To be notified on GPSC website

application_fee.details: Once the application form is filled appropriately, candidates are required to pay the application fee either online or offline. Applications where fee payment has been made after the last date will be summarily rejected and no refund of this payment will be provided. Only after successful payment can candidates download their application form. Candidates should carefully read the "Instructions" on the Commission's website for the exact fee amount applicable to their category.

age_limit.age_details: Knowledge of Konkani is essential for most posts; Knowledge of Marathi is desirable

qualification.details: Candidates must fulfill all eligibility conditions before applying. Knowledge of Konkani is essential for most posts. In case of non-availability of suitable candidates with knowledge of Konkani, the GPSC may recommend a candidate if otherwise found fit and this requirement can be relaxed by the Government on the recommendation of GPSC, if the Government is of the opinion that it is necessary or expedient so to do in case of Group 'A' posts in Professional Colleges and Consultants in the Directorate of Health Services. Qualifications are relaxable at the discretion of the GPSC in the case of candidates otherwise well qualified. | Educational Qualification - Post-wise

vacancy_details.Post Name (Lecturer in): Lecturer in Community Dentistry/Public Health Dentistry

category_wise_vacancy.Post Name (Lecturer in): Lecturer in Community Dentistry/Public Health Dentistry

selection_process: Stage 1 – Shortlisting Applications screened based on educational qualifications and eligibility. Where applications are large, Commission will shortlist candidates as per Instructions on its website.
Stage 2 – Interview Shortlisted candidates called for Interview. Date and details will be communicated via email/mobile/Commission's website.
Prescribed Educational Qualifications are minimum and mere possession of the same does not entitle candidates to be called for Interview. Where the number of applications received is large

the Commission will shortlist candidates to be called for Interview as mentioned in the "Instructions" available on the Commission's website. All communications concerning the candidate's application will be sent through the email address/mobile number provided in the application form. Candidates are advised to check their email accounts
and the Commission's website regularly for updates/notices concerning their application.
how_to_apply: Candidates must apply online only through the Commission's website at https://gpsc.goa.gov.in or the Government of Goa portal at https://cbes.goa.gov.in/. Candidates should carefully read the "Instructions" available on the Commission's website before filling the application online. The email address and mobile number of the candidate should invariably be provided in the application form. All candidates are advised to check their email accounts/mobile phones/Commission's website regularly for updates/notices concerning their application.

The last date for filling up applications for all posts other than the posts by transfer on deputation is 22.05.2026. The last date for filling up applications for transfer on deputation posts only is 22.06.2026. If any discrepancy is observed in the translated version of the advertisement, the English version will be considered as authentic.
Apply Online at: https://gpsc.goa.gov.in OR https://cbes.goa.gov.in/
important_instructions: Candidates must apply online only - refer to Commission's website https://gpsc.goa.gov.in or Government of Goa portal https://cbes.goa.gov.in/. Carefully read the "Instructions" available on the Commission's website before filling the application online. The last date for all posts (except Transfer on Deputation posts) is 22.05.2026; for Transfer on Deputation posts only, the last date is 22.06.2026. Application fee must be paid on or before the last date - applications where fee payment is made after the last date will be summarily rejected and no refund will be provided. For offline payment: e-challan is valid for 7 days from generation; Bank stamp is essential; stamped e-challan must be produced to the Commission's office on or before the last date. Only after successful fee payment can candidates download their application form. Email address and mobile number must invariably be provided in the application form - all communications will be sent through these. Candidates are advised to check their email accounts, mobile phones, and the Commission's website regularly for updates/notices. Prescribed Educational Qualifications are minimum - mere possession does not entitle candidates to be called for Interview. Knowledge of Konkani is essential for most posts; Knowledge of Marathi is desirable. This requirement may be relaxed by the Government on the recommendation of GPSC in specified cases. Qualifications are relaxable at the discretion of GPSC in case of candidates otherwise well qualified. Experience requirements are relaxable for SC/ST candidates at the discretion of GPSC in specified circumstances. If any discrepancy is observed in the translated version of advertisement, the English version will be considered as authentic.

faq.question: Q1. What is Goa PSC Advertisement No. 05 Year 2026 recruitment about?
faq.answer: Goa Public Service Commission (GPSC), Panaji, has released Advertisement No. 05 Year 2026 dated 08 May 2026 for recruitment to various Group A and Group B posts in the Department of Environment and Climate Change, Public Health Department (Goa Dental College & Hospital and Institute of Psychiatry and Human Behaviour), Town and Country Planning Department, and Department of Personnel.

faq.question: Q2. What are the important dates for Goa PSC Advertisement No. 05/2026?
faq.answer: The advertisement is published on 08 May 2026, the last date to apply online for all posts other than transfer on deputation is 22 May 2026, and the last date to apply online for transfer on deputation posts under the Department of Environment and Climate Change is 22 June 2026.

faq.question: Q3. Which posts are included under Goa PSC Recruitment 2026 Advertisement No. 05/2026?
faq.answer: The notification includes Scientific Officer, Assistant Director, Assistant Engineer, Environmental Officer, and Dy. Director (Technical) by transfer on deputation in the Department of Environment and Climate Change, Lecturer posts in various Dental specialities at Goa Dental College & Hospital, Psychiatrist and Clinical Psychologist at the Institute of Psychiatry and Human Behaviour, Dy. Town Planner in the Town and Country Planning Department, and Block Development Officer in the Department of Personnel.

faq.question: Q4. What is the eligibility and qualification requirement for Goa PSC Advertisement No. 05/2026 posts?
faq.answer: Eligibility conditions are post‑specific and include requirements such as analogous posts on regular basis for deputation posts, dental qualifications with PG in relevant speciality for Lecturer posts, medical qualification with PG in Psychiatry for Psychiatrist, Master’s degree with M.Phil/clinical psychology diploma for Clinical Psychologist, degree in Regional/Town Planning with relevant experience for Dy. Town Planner, and any degree with knowledge of Konkani for Block Development Officer, along with other experience and desirable qualifications as stated in the official notification.

faq.question: Q5. Is knowledge of Konkani and Marathi compulsory for Goa PSC Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Knowledge of Konkani is essential for most Goa PSC posts notified under Advertisement No. 05 Year 2026, while knowledge of Marathi is desirable and may be listed as a desirable qualification for certain posts; in specific Group A professional and consultant posts, the Government may relax the Konkani requirement on the recommendation of GPSC if suitable candidates are not available.

faq.question: Q6. What is the age limit for Goa PSC Advertisement No. 05/2026 posts?
faq.answer: For most direct recruitment posts such as Lecturers, Psychiatrists, Clinical Psychologists, Dy. Town Planner, and Block Development Officer, the upper age limit is generally not exceeding 45 years as mentioned in the notification, while deputation posts follow the rules applicable for transfer on deputation and existing pay matrix levels.

faq.question: Q7. How can I apply online for Goa PSC Recruitment 2026 (Advt. 05/2026)?
faq.answer: Candidates have to apply online only through the official Goa Public Service Commission website by accessing the online application link or via the Government of Goa CBES portal, filling in all required details, uploading documents, and paying the application fee within the prescribed last date.

faq.question: Q8. What is the application fee and payment process for Goa PSC Advertisement No. 05 Year 2026?
faq.answer: Applicants must pay the prescribed application fee either through online payment on the portal or via offline mode using e‑challan at any SBI branch in Goa, where the e‑challan remains valid for a limited period (generally 7 days) and the stamped challan must reach the Commission’s office on or before the last date; applications with fee paid after the last date are liable to be rejected with no refund.
